Mtn To Compel Re Special Interoggs; To Compel Re: Rfp No 19; To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN M. GATTI OF MANATT LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 312-4169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MORGAN JACKSON OF FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP, TEL. NO. 954-4400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
TARA L. WILLIAMS OF AIKEN WELCH COURT REPORTERS, TEL. NO. (510) 451-1580, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOACHIM BERGMANN, ALEX ZWERDLING AND BERGMANN ZWERDLING DIRECT's Motion To Compel Re Special Interrogatories; To Compel Re: RFP No 19; To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce And Monetary Sanctions -
Defendants' motions to compel and produce documents are denied in part and granted in part as follows:
1) The motion is denied as to Bergmann Zwerdling Direct Inc.'s Second Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 20-221, Bergmann's First Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 1-198, and Zwerdling's First Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 1-198. The court finds:
(a) The moving party failed to sustain its burden as to the necessity for the large number of interrogatories in view of the provisions of C.C.P. Sections 2030.030, 2030.040, and 2030.050.
(b) The special interrogatories seek duplicate information. To the extent that the interrogatories concern 66 discrete documents, each document does not require multiple interrogatories concerning the plaintiff's contentions.
2) The motion to compel production of documents in response to Request No. 19 is granted to the extent that plaintiff failed to produce the responsive documents that plaintiff agreed to produce as reflected in the court's prior order of November 15, 2013 (directing further meet and confer discussions). However, if all such documents have already been produced, then plaintiff must alternatively provide a declaration under penalty of perjury certifying that such production has been made.
3) The motion to compel production of documents in response to request numbers 1-4, 5-12, 14-18 and 21-29 is denied in part and granted in part. The motion is denied insofar as the original objections as to breadth and relevance appear well taken, and the correspondence fails to demonstrate that the parties had reached a comprehensive agreement for production of all documents so described. Plaintiff's supplemental responses do not reflect an agreement other than for "non-privileged" documents "if any."
The motion is granted to the extent that plaintiff agreed to produce, "communications made by Mr. Baughmann to Plaintiff's actual or potential clients, omitting any copyrighted direct mail pieces attached to any such communications." If all such documents have already been produced, then plaintiff must alternatively provide a declaration under penalty of perjury certifying that such production has been made.
4) The requests for sanctions by both sets of parties are denied. As to the moving parties, the objections were substantially justified. As to the opposing party, sanctions are denied on the ground that imposition of sanctions would otherwise be unjust. Plaintiff's failure to obtain a protective order previously, to move for a proposed protective order, or to provide the court with a trade secrets designation (pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2019.210) limits the court's ability to provide restrictions on discovery or specify alternative discovery methods under C.C.P. Section 2019.030. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR # 13428 =302/EG
